title: RVT.BSP.004.CONSUMPTION_RECORDED — Envelope consumption recorded
description: RUNTIME CONTRACT. Every payload published on the bus by CAP.BSP.004.ENV with routing key 'EVT.BSP.004.ENVELOPE_CONSUMED.RVT.BSP.004.CONSUMPTION_RECORDED' MUST validate against this schema. Per ADR-TECH-STRAT-001 Rule 3, the payload is a DOMAIN EVENT DDD: atomic transition data of a single envelope debit emitted by AGG.BSP.004.ENV.PERIOD_BUDGET on every successful CMD.RECORD_CONSUMPTION. It is NOT a snapshot of the read model RES.BSP.004.OPEN_ENVELOPE and NOT a field patch — it is a coherent state-change record. Per INV.ENV.004 of the aggregate, when the same debit causes an envelope to reach its cap, RVT.BSP.004.ENVELOPE_CAP_REACHED is also emitted in the same transaction (paired event); receiving consumers must tolerate that pairing. AVAILABLE BALANCE DERIVATION (consumer-side convenience): available_balance = cap_amount - consumed_amount. Equivalently, on this wire-level event: 'remaining_amount' field already represents (cap_amount - consumed_amount_after) at the moment of the transition. The 'cap_amount' itself is NOT transported on this event — it is part of RES.BSP.004.OPEN_ENVELOPE state held by consumers (sourced from the prior RVT.BSP.004.ENVELOPE_INITIALIZED message, allocation_id-correlated). Consumers maintaining the open-envelope read model recompute their displayed balance from 'consumed_amount_after' and the cap_amount they already hold for that allocation_id. CORRELATION KEY: 'case_id'. Resolution path: consumers wishing to obtain the canonical beneficiary identifier (OBJ.REF.001.BENEFICIARY_RECORD.internal_id) MUST query CAP.REF.001.BEN with 'case_id'. The producer does NOT carry the canonical beneficiary identifier on the bus (privacy + decoupling per ADR-BCM-URBA-0009).

event_id.description: Unique identifier of this domain event occurrence. Stable across replays; suitable for idempotency keys at the consumer side. The aggregate enforces at-most-once application of any (event_id, command-kind) pair (INV.ENV.007).

occurred_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp at which the consumption was recorded by AGG.BSP.004.ENV.PERIOD_BUDGET (transition timestamp — not the bus publish time).

case_id.description: CORRELATION KEY. Reference to the participation case (owned by CAP.BSP.002.ENR). Resolution path to OBJ.REF.001.BENEFICIARY_RECORD.internal_id is the consumer's responsibility — query CAP.REF.001.BEN with this value. The producer never carries 'internal_id' on the wire (privacy + decoupling per ADR-BCM-URBA-0009).

period_index.description: Integer index of the budget period this debit belongs to. Advances on each closure-then-reopen cycle (ADR-BCM-FUNC-0008). Combined with 'case_id', identifies the AGG.BSP.004.ENV.PERIOD_BUDGET aggregate instance that emitted the event.

allocation_id.description: Identifier of the inner envelope (one per spending category) targeted by the debit. Stable for the lifetime of the envelope; used by consumer read-models (RES.BSP.004.OPEN_ENVELOPE) to correlate with the prior RVT.BSP.004.ENVELOPE_INITIALIZED event and recover 'cap_amount'.

category.description: Spending category of the envelope (e.g. ALIMENTATION, TRANSPORT). The canonical source of category sets per tier is CAP.REF.001.TIE; the producer redundantly carries 'category' on the wire to spare consumers a join.

amount.description: Debit amount applied by THIS consumption (transition delta, not a cumulative). Always strictly positive (INV.ENV.003 rejects amount <= 0).

consumed_amount_after.description: Total 'consumed_amount' of the envelope AFTER applying this debit. Domain-event semantics: this is the post-transition value of the inner envelope's consumed_amount field (OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION.consumed_amount). Consumers maintaining a read model overwrite their previous value with this one (last-writer-wins keyed on (allocation_id, occurred_at)).

remaining_amount.description: Equal to (cap_amount - consumed_amount_after). Zero when the envelope is exactly capped (in which case INV.ENV.004 also emits RVT.BSP.004.ENVELOPE_CAP_REACHED in the same transaction). NOTE: the available-balance formula 'cap_amount - consumed_amount' (per OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION definition) is materialised here to spare consumers a join with the cap_amount they hold for that allocation_id. cap_amount itself is NOT transported on this event — it is sourced by consumers from the prior RVT.BSP.004.ENVELOPE_INITIALIZED message of the same allocation_id.

transaction_id.description: Reference to the underlying card transaction that triggered this debit (for traceability across capabilities). Optional — the upstream transaction-authorised resource event may not always carry one.

causation_event_id.description: Identifier of the upstream event (e.g. RVT.BSP.004.PAYMENT_GRANTED) that caused this consumption. Optional — propagated for end-to-end traceability when available.
